summary:
The codon for selenocysteine is recognized as an exception during translation due to its post-termination insertion process involving the normally silent UGA codon; it does not have one like other standard amino acids but rather relies on specific mechanisms triggered by this particular sequence. \boxed{\text{specially processed UGA}} 

The summary captures both critical aspects—its unusual nature within coding sequences and how it’s integrated through non-standard means—and aligns with common educational content about genetic codes and protein synthesis processes. This response directly addresses what was asked while adhering strictly to two concise statements focused solely around clinically significant details pertinent to understanding selenocysteine inclusion into proteins.
